arguments.name: Apple Corporate Intranet
arguments.description: Apple's corporate intranet hub designed to reflect the brand’s emphasis on privacy, premium quality, and human-centered design. It serves as the definitive internal hub for leadership communications, governance, HR, R&D, ESG, and regional operations.
arguments.welcome_message: Welcome to Apple Corporate Intranet. This hub anchors the global Apple workforce—from Cupertino to campuses worldwide—with trusted information, clear guidance, and a space that mirrors Apple’s commitment to privacy, premium quality, and human-centered design.

arguments.name: Apple Newsroom
arguments.description: Official Apple communications channel for employees: quarterly results, product announcements, and leadership memos. This channel sets the cadence for透明 and precise updates, ensuring alignment across the global workforce.

arguments.name: Leadership & Strategy
arguments.description: Tim Cook and the executive team share strategic direction, governance updates, and leadership communications. This channel provides a window into how Apple translates plans into action across the organization.

frequent_questions.question: Who can post here?
frequent_questions.answer: Senior leaders and approved communications staff only.

frequent_questions.question: How often are updates posted?
frequent_questions.answer: On a cadence aligned to quarterly planning and major announcements.

frequent_questions.question: Can employees comment on leadership posts?
frequent_questions.answer: Comments are managed confidentially by the Communications team.

arguments.name: People & Culture
arguments.description: HR policies, diversity initiatives, learning resources, and employee programs that empower Apple employees to grow within a privacy-first culture.

frequent_questions.question: How do I request tuition reimbursement?
frequent_questions.answer: Submit through the Learning portal with HR approval.

frequent_questions.question: What is the parental leave policy?
frequent_questions.answer: Parental leave details are outlined in the HR policy page; eligibility and duration are per local law.

frequent_questions.question: How can I join an Employee Resource Group (ERG)?
frequent_questions.answer: Join through People & Culture ERG page and request access from HR.

arguments.name: ESG & Corporate Responsibility
arguments.description: Environmental progress, privacy commitments, and social impact programs—communicating progress toward carbon neutrality and responsible stewardship.

frequent_questions.question: What are Apple’s carbon-neutral targets for 2030?
frequent_questions.answer: The plan covers the supply chain and product lifecycle with milestones published in the Environmental Progress Report.

frequent_questions.question: How does Apple protect user privacy?
frequent_questions.answer: On-device processing, differential privacy, and ATT framework are core elements.

frequent_questions.question: How can employees participate in ESG programs?
frequent_questions.answer: Through the Apple Impact Accelerator and REJI programs.

arguments.title: Q4 FY2024 Results: Apple Reports Revenue of $391.0B, Up 2%
arguments.body:
Q4 FY2024 Results: Apple Reports Revenue of $391.0B, Up 2%

Apple today reported strong momentum across its portfolio for the September quarter, with total revenue of $391.0 billion, up 2% year over year. The result reflects resilient demand for core products and services, as well as disciplined execution across supply chain and operations. Gross margin stood at 46.2%, underscoring efficiency amid a broad product and services mix. Operating income reached $123.2 billion, with net income of $93.7 billion, highlighting Apple’s ability to convert demand into durable profitability.

Split across product categories, the iPhone remains the primary revenue driver, contributing a substantial share of total revenue while Services continue to expand in scale. The company continues to balance high-end hardware launches with subscription services, ensuring a cohesive ecosystem that emphasizes privacy and on-device processing.

Operationally, Apple's geographic footprint remains global, supported by 516 Apple Stores worldwide and a vast network of supply partners. The company’s workforce of approximately 160,000 full-time employees is supported by a robust infrastructure that spans design, engineering, manufacturing, and customer care. Apple Park in Cupertino remains the symbolic HQ, with key operations distributed across campuses in Austin, Seattle, New York, and international locations.

During the year, Apple advanced several strategic initiatives: the Vision Pro platform and spatial-computing ecosystem expanded in parallel with ongoing Apple Silicon development. The company also delivered on-device AI and privacy-centric features across iOS, iPadOS, and macOS, reinforcing a product and experience that prioritizes user trust. These investments underpin a long-term plan to sustain leadership in premium design and technology.

Looking ahead, Apple will continue to navigate a dynamic technology landscape by pairing breakthrough products with disciplined execution and rigorous governance. The leadership team remains focused on delivering value to customers, employees, and shareholders while maintaining Apple’s uncompromising stance on privacy and user-centric design. Read the full report at investor.apple.com

arguments.title: Vision Pro Debuts: Spatial Computing on the Global Stage
arguments.body:
Vision Pro Debuts: Spatial Computing on the Global Stage

The Vision Pro platform marks a new era for Apple in spatial computing. With a launch price of $3,499, Vision Pro extends Apple’s hardware and software ecosystem into immersive experiences. The device sets the stage for a broader %{Vision os%} ecosystem, pairing on-device processing with a privacy-first cloud strategy. The debut complements ongoing Apple Silicon work across iPhone, iPad, and Mac families, and aligns with the company’s focus on premium design and user privacy.

Developers are invited to participate in the ecosystem through developer tools and advanced APIs, enabling a richer, more connected experience across devices. Apple’s retail and regional teams will support market rollout across key geographies, including North America and Europe, with a staged global expansion plan.

arguments.title: iPhone 16 Launch Recap: Camera Control Button and A18
arguments.body:
iPhone 16 Launch Recap: Camera Control Button and A18

Apple introduced the iPhone 16 with a renewed focus on camera capabilities, including a new camera control button and the powerful A18 chip. The launch reinforces Apple’s strategy of integrating high-end hardware with an accessible software experience. The event highlighted improvements in photography, battery life, and processing efficiency, contributing to sustained demand across iPhone and ecosystem services.

As with prior launches, supply-chain and regional market readiness remained central to planning. Apple’s product cadence continues to balance premium devices with strong privacy and on-device processing features that protect user data.

arguments.title: Apple Intelligence: AI Across iOS, iPadOS, macOS
arguments.body:
Apple Intelligence: AI Across iOS, iPadOS, macOS

Apple Intelligence brings on-device AI capabilities to the entire OS family, delivering smarter experiences while preserving privacy. The approach emphasizes on-device processing, differential privacy, and privacy-preserving machine learning across apps and services. This recap outlines how Apple’s AI features integrate with existing hardware and software, and what it means for developers and end-users.

With a focus on user trust and seamless experiences, the initiative complements Vision Pro, Vision OS, and silicon advancements, reinforcing Apple’s commitment to privacy-centric innovation.

arguments.title: Pride@Apple: Broad-Based Inclusion Initiatives
arguments.body:
Pride@Apple: Broad-Based Inclusion Initiatives

As part of Apple’s ongoing commitment to diversity and inclusion, Pride@Apple activities highlight employee resource groups, mentorship programs, and inclusive events across regions. Participation metrics show growing engagement and impact on recruitment and retention. This article shares program milestones, leadership statements, and opportunities for involvement across Apple campuses.

Apple remains focused on building a culture where every employee can bring their whole self to work, aligning with the company’s values around privacy and human-centered design.

arguments.title: HR Policy Update: Parental Leave and Education Reimbursement
arguments.body:
HR Policy Update: Parental Leave and Education Reimbursement

Apple updates its HR policies to clarify parental leave eligibility and expand education reimbursement options. This article outlines who qualifies, duration limits, and the reimbursement process, with references to local regulation considerations across regions. It also reinforces our commitment to supporting employees through key life moments and professional development.

Employees are encouraged to review the updated policy and confirm understanding; managers are asked to acknowledge policy changes with their teams.

arguments.title: Apple Park Modernization: Workplace Experience
arguments.body:
Apple Park Modernization: Workplace Experience

Apple Park, Cupertino, continues to evolve as a symbol of the company’s design ethos and employee experience. This update covers workspace renovations, improved collaboration zones, and enhanced wellness facilities. The project aligns with Apple’s commitment to sustainability and privacy-first operations.

Regional teams collaborate to optimize the occupancy strategy and ensure that Apple’s most iconic campus remains a thriving hub for innovation.

arguments.title: Regional Hubs: AI & ML Centers in Seattle and Tokyo
arguments.body:
Regional Hubs: AI & ML Centers in Seattle and Tokyo

Apple’s AI & ML capabilities are advancing through regional hubs in Seattle and Tokyo. This article highlights local talent, partnerships with universities, and cross-regional collaboration that accelerates product development while maintaining stringent privacy and security standards.

Regional teams are collaborating with global peers to accelerate responsible AI deployment across devices, services, and experiences.

arguments.title: Global Store and Campus Openings: 2024 Milestones
arguments.body:
Global Store and Campus Openings: 2024 Milestones

Apple’s footprint expands with new stores and campus openings across key regions. The article outlines milestones in Europe, Asia, and the Americas, and how these expansions support a premium customer and employee experience aligned with Apple’s privacy-centric philosophy.

Apple Park and new campuses continue to be central to Apple’s global operations, with a focus on sustainable design and employee well-being.
